summary: A medieval-style stable or barn structure featuring a central dark-roofed main building with a gable roof, flanked by two tall wooden towers with overhanging eaves. The building has a mixed material construction with stonebrick foundation, yellow/tan brick walls, and dark wood roofing. A perimeter fence of stonebrick runs around the entire structure.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main structure with two prominent corner towers and surrounding fence perimeter
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry with towers at opposite corners

materials.name: wood
materials.role: towers, roof structure, and overhanging eaves

rebuild_hints: Build central rectangular structure approximately 16x14 blocks
Construct two tall wooden towers at opposite corners, approximately 2-3 blocks wide each
Add overhanging wooden eaves/slabs extending 2-3 blocks from tower tops
Create gable roof with dark wood blocks, stepping inward toward center
Use stonebrick for foundation layer and complete perimeter fence 1 block high
Add yellow/tan brick as primary wall material between foundation and roof
Place small window openings on tower faces
Ensure symmetrical placement of towers on diagonal corners
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and room divisions not visible from exterior views
Precise block-by-block roof construction pattern unclear due to angle
Whether towers are solid or hollow structures
Exact height of fence perimeter relative to main structure
Interior floor count and layout configuration
